---
title: "A Field Guide to Statistics and Biostatistics Journals"
subtitle: "For students in semiparametric statistics, causal inference, and targeted learning"
author: "Compiled for UC Berkeley Biostatistics & the Center for Targeted Machine Learning and Causal Inference"
date: today
---

# Purpose of this guide

This document is a working map of the journal landscape for students and early-career researchers in biostatistics, causal inference, and semiparametric theory — particularly those affiliated with the UC Berkeley Division of Biostatistics and the Center for Targeted Machine Learning and Causal Inference (CTML). It is meant to help you:

- Decide where to submit a given paper
- Understand what each venue's editorial culture and audience actually rewards
- Develop taste for which venues are prestigious within our subfield (which does not always track bibliometrics)
- Think strategically about the tenure clock, the job market, and the arc of an early career

Tier lists are inherently opinionated. Treat the rankings as a starting point for conversation with your advisor, not as fact. Fit matters more than tier; a well-placed paper at a specialty journal that reaches your actual audience is worth more than a grudgingly accepted paper at a generalist one.

---

# The tier list at a glance

## Main ranking (semiparametrics and causal inference lens)

```{=html}
<div class="tier-row">
  <div class="tier-label tier-s-label">S</div>
  <div class="tier-body">
    <div class="tier-caption">Where the canon gets written</div>
    <div class="tier-journals">
      <span class="journal-pill tier-s-pill">Annals of Statistics</span>
      <span class="journal-pill tier-s-pill">JASA (T&amp;M)</span>
      <span class="journal-pill tier-s-pill">JRSS-B</span>
      <span class="journal-pill tier-s-pill">Biometrika</span>
    </div>
  </div>
</div>

<div class="tier-row">
  <div class="tier-label tier-a-label">A</div>
  <div class="tier-body">
    <div class="tier-caption">Core causal/semiparametric homes</div>
    <div class="tier-journals">
      <span class="journal-pill tier-a-pill">Biometrics</span>
      <span class="journal-pill tier-a-pill">Biostatistics</span>
      <span class="journal-pill tier-a-pill">JASA (A&amp;CS)</span>
      <span class="journal-pill tier-a-pill">Epidemiology</span>
      <span class="journal-pill tier-a-pill">J. Econometrics</span>
      <span class="journal-pill tier-a-pill">JMLR</span>
      <span class="journal-pill tier-a-pill">Econometrica</span>
      <span class="journal-pill tier-a-pill">Int. J. Biostatistics</span>
      <span class="journal-pill tier-a-pill">J. Causal Inference</span>
    </div>
  </div>
</div>

<div class="tier-row">
  <div class="tier-label tier-b-label">B</div>
  <div class="tier-body">
    <div class="tier-caption">Strong &amp; relevant, often audience-specific</div>
    <div class="tier-journals">
      <span class="journal-pill tier-b-pill">AoAS</span>
      <span class="journal-pill tier-b-pill">Am. J. Epidemiology</span>
      <span class="journal-pill tier-b-pill">Statistical Science</span>
      <span class="journal-pill tier-b-pill">Statistics in Medicine</span>
      <span class="journal-pill tier-b-pill">Bernoulli</span>
      <span class="journal-pill tier-b-pill">JRSS-A</span>
      <span class="journal-pill tier-b-pill">Rev. Econ. &amp; Stat.</span>
      <span class="journal-pill tier-b-pill">Observational Studies</span>
      <span class="journal-pill tier-b-pill">Electronic J. Stat.</span>
      <span class="journal-pill tier-b-pill">Pharmaceutical Statistics</span>
    </div>
  </div>
</div>

<div class="tier-row">
  <div class="tier-label tier-c-label">C</div>
  <div class="tier-body">
    <div class="tier-caption">Respectable niche / methods-applied outlets</div>
    <div class="tier-journals">
      <span class="journal-pill tier-c-pill">Stat. Methods Med. Res.</span>
      <span class="journal-pill tier-c-pill">J. Clinical Epidemiology</span>
      <span class="journal-pill tier-c-pill">Health Services Research</span>
      <span class="journal-pill tier-c-pill">J. Health Economics</span>
      <span class="journal-pill tier-c-pill">Lifetime Data Analysis</span>
      <span class="journal-pill tier-c-pill">Scand. J. Stat.</span>
      <span class="journal-pill tier-c-pill">Econometric Theory</span>
      <span class="journal-pill tier-c-pill">CSDA</span>
      <span class="journal-pill tier-c-pill">JRSS-C</span>
      <span class="journal-pill tier-c-pill">Biometrical Journal</span>
    </div>
  </div>
</div>
```

## ML conferences (parallel track)

```{=html}
<div class="tier-row">
  <div class="tier-label tier-conf-label">ML</div>
  <div class="tier-body">
    <div class="tier-caption">Peer-reviewed venues where much causal-ML work now lives</div>
    <div class="tier-journals">
      <span class="journal-pill tier-conf-pill">NeurIPS</span>
      <span class="journal-pill tier-conf-pill">ICML</span>
      <span class="journal-pill tier-conf-pill">AISTATS</span>
      <span class="journal-pill tier-conf-pill">ICLR</span>
      <span class="journal-pill tier-conf-pill">UAI</span>
      <span class="journal-pill tier-conf-pill">COLT</span>
      <span class="journal-pill tier-conf-pill">CLeaR</span>
    </div>
  </div>
</div>
```

---

## How to read this guide

- [**What makes a journal prestigious**](prestige.qmd) — the dimensions that actually matter when we say one journal is "better" than another
- **Tier pages** — detailed notes on each venue:
  - [S tier](s-tier.qmd) — the field-defining flagships
  - [A tier](a-tier.qmd) — core homes for semiparametric and causal work
  - [B tier](b-tier.qmd) — strong and relevant, often audience-specific
  - [C tier](c-tier.qmd) — respectable niche and methods-applied outlets
- [**ML conferences**](ml-conferences.qmd) — the parallel CS-culture track
- [**Strategy & practice**](strategy.qmd) — submission strategy, R&R, the tenure clock, mentorship
